message:
fix(dog): auto-clear hung dogs and orphan sessions in health checker

Dogs that finish work but fail to call `gt dog done` sit at an idle
Claude Code prompt forever, consuming ~500MB RAM each. The health
checker detected these as AgentHung but only reported them (ZFC).

Now when --auto-clear is set, hung dogs (idle > maxInactivity) and
orphan sessions (state=idle but tmux alive) are killed and reclaimed.
Default maxInactivity lowered from 30m to 10m to match typical 5m
plugin timeouts.

Closes gt-09e

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
